name: pit-flag-value-forms
notes: Operational pitfall: flag-form drift (`--flag=value` vs `--flag value`) for the same value-taking CLI flag must not split a single logical invocation into two streams. If a space-separated value leaked into the selector, run B's stream key would diverge from run A's and previous-comparable would abstain with a spurious `baseline-missing` even though both invocations select the same `unit` vitest project. The command/selector splitter normalizes both forms to the same canonical `--project=unit` command token, so B's automatic previous-comparable resolves A as its baseline (exact). A marker commit between A and B keeps the two runs content-distinct (different tree) so the store does not collapse them to a single idempotent run_id (§3.5/§5.8), letting the baseline-resolution proof stand on its own record.

steps.message: advance tree before B (keep A and B content-distinct)
